response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong full-stack, testing discipline, and some systems-level work (DLang low-latency services at Sociomantic, C/C++ at National Instruments), plus GIS experience via GISCollective. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust as primary, and the resume shows only brief, dated C/C++ exposure (2014-2015) and no Rust. Recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js. Could be a stretch hire if the team values systems mindset and geospatial background over current C++ fluency.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map
Strong systems background: low-latency DLang microservices handling millions of req/s
Solid engineering discipline — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing workshops
concerns: No modern C++ experience in last ~10 years; C/C++ work at NI was brief (2014-2015)
Recent stack is TypeScript/Node/React — far from the C++ routing pipeline target
